preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the voiceless post-alveolar affricate [tʃ] (represented here as 'ch') is a form of palatalization and affrication, typically occurring when the velar sound is influenced by following high front vowels.

examples.note: Development of palatal affricates from historical velar fricatives and stops before [i] and [y] (e.g., in the Pinyin 'q' series).
Widespread palatalization of velars in the development of Romance languages (e.g., Latin 'centum').
